title: Shri Shirdi Sai Baba Temple
intro: Shirdi, the home of the revered saint Sai Baba, is one of the most significant spiritual destinations in India.
description:
Shri Shirdi Sai Baba Temple – Shirdi, Maharashtra
Introduction
Shirdi, the home of the revered saint Sai Baba, is one of the most significant spiritual destinations in India. The Shirdi Sai Baba Temple, known as the Samadhi Mandir, attracts millions of devotees from across the globe. Sai Baba, who lived here for many years, preached the principles of unity, love, and compassion, famously emphasizing "Shraddha" (Faith) and "Saburi" (Patience).
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Saibaba Samadhi Mandir
 Location: Shirdi, Ahmednagar District, Maharashtra 423109
 Presiding Deity: Shri Sai Baba
 Significance: A major spiritual center promoting harmony and faith.
History and Significance
Shirdi was the Karmabhoomi of Sai Baba, who arrived here as a young man and stayed until his Mahasamadhi in 1918. The temple is built over Baba’s tomb, and the serene atmosphere continues to draw people looking for solace, spiritual guidance, and blessings.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air:
 Shirdi International Airport: The nearest airport, well-connected to major Indian cities.
 Pune/Aurangabad/Mumbai: Alternative airports for those traveling long distances.
🚆 By Train:
 Sainagar Shirdi Railway Station: This station is well-connected to major cities. Taxis and autos are available to reach the temple complex.
🛣️ By Road:
 Shirdi is well-connected by road to Pune, Mumbai, Nashik, and Aurangabad via state transport buses, private luxury buses, and taxis.
Best Time to Visit
 October to March: The weather is cool and pleasant for pilgrimage.
 Special Note: Thursdays are considered very auspicious for visiting the temple.
Temple Timings
🕒 General Timings: The temple is generally open from early morning (around 4:00 AM) until late night (around 10:30 PM).
(Note: Darshan timings are subject to change during festivals.)
Main Festivals
 Guru Purnima: Celebrated with immense fervor by devotees.
 Dussehra (Vijayadashami): Marks the anniversary of Baba's Mahasamadhi.
 Ram Navami: A major festival celebrated at the temple.
Nearby Attractions
 Dwarkamai: The mosque where Sai Baba lived; holds immense historical and spiritual significance.
 Chavadi: Where Baba used to sleep on alternate nights.
 Lendi Baug: A garden sanctified by Baba, where he used to sit near a lamp.
 Khandoba Temple: Where Baba was first welcomed and called "Sai."
Dress Code
 Devotees are encouraged to wear modest and clean traditional clothing.
Facilities for Devotees
 Darshan Queue: An organized system for Mukha Darshan.
 Prasadalaya: A massive, modern dining hall providing hygienic and affordable meals (Prasad).
 Accommodation: Various Bhakta Niwas and guest houses managed by the Shri Saibaba Sansthan Trust.
Conclusion
A visit to Shirdi is a journey of peace and surrender. The teachings of Sai Baba, rooted in simplicity and love, resonate through the air of Shirdi, providing every visitor with a sense of calm and spiritual clarity.

faq.q1: Where is the Shirdi Sai Baba Temple located?
faq.a1: It is located in Shirdi, Ahmednagar district, Maharashtra.
faq.q2: What was Sai Baba’s main message?
faq.a2: Sai Baba’s message was — "Shraddha and Saburi" (Faith and Patience).
faq.q3: What is the best time to visit Shirdi?
faq.a3: The period from October to March is considered ideal for travel.
faq.q4: Which places should one visit in Shirdi?
faq.a4: Dwarkamai, Chavadi, and Lendi Baug are the major places to visit.
